mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 7.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 7.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 900,000 units signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 900,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 98.7 signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability and reduces inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 98.7 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 13725.7 billion signals a strengthening economy that boosts consumer spending and corporate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 13725.7 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial unemployment claims below 140,000 signals a strengthening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 140,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 422,794 million signals a strengthening economy that boosts consumer spending and corporate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 422,794 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
